description: JS/Python preprocessor parity corpus. Used by tools/parity_check.py and the .github/workflows/parity.yml CI gate. Drawn from evals/novel_cases.json plus hand-picked preprocessor-targeted cases. See tests/corpus/README.md for selection criteria.

cases.input: Your session has been terminated due to an authentication timeout.
cases.note: Different jargon than the library example. Tests whether the agent flags technical language beyond the specific phrase 'authorization parameters.'

cases.input: You were signed out because you were away too long. Sign in again to continue.
cases.note: Plain language version of the same concept. Should pass cleanly.

cases.input: Your API rate limit has been exceeded. Retry after 60 seconds.
cases.note: Technical language, but the audience is developers. Tests whether the agent respects 'unless the audience requires it.'

cases.input: As part of our ongoing commitment to improving the platform experience for all users, we've added dark mode to your settings.
cases.note: Buries the key info (dark mode is available) behind filler. Different pattern than the library discount example.

cases.input: When you change your password you will be signed out of all devices and will need to sign back in on each one using your new password within the next 24 hours.
cases.note: Single sentence at ~30 words. Tests the 15-20 word threshold with a run-on that chains consequences.

cases.input: Changing your password signs you out everywhere. Sign back in with your new password.
cases.note: Same idea, two short sentences. Should pass.

cases.input: You can't proceed without verifying your email.
cases.note: 'Can't without' is technically a double negative but reads naturally. The revised rule (CLR-05) explicitly allows this. Should pass.

cases.input: The report was generated by the analytics engine at 3:00 AM.
cases.note: Passive voice in a status message. Under the revised VT-01, passive is acceptable for system status messages where the actor is irrelevant. Should pass.

cases.input: The analytics engine generated your report at 3:00 AM.
cases.note: Active version of the same sentence.

cases.input: Yikes! Looks like your file got totally wrecked. My bad — wanna try again?
cases.note: Different casual language than 'Oopsie!' Tests whether the agent recognizes a range of overly casual patterns.

cases.input: SYSTEM NOTICE: File processing terminated. Reinitiate upload sequence.
cases.note: Tests the other end of VT-03 — too robotic rather than too casual.

cases.input: We ran into a problem uploading your file. Try uploading it again.
cases.note: Conversational but professional. Should pass.

cases.input: It seems like your changes might have been saved, but you may want to double-check just in case.
cases.note: Stacks hedges: 'seems like,' 'might have,' 'may want to,' 'just in case.' Different phrasing than the library example.

cases.input: Your changes are saved.
cases.note: Direct, confident statement. Under the revised VT-01, passive voice is acceptable for confirmations. Should pass for both VT-01 and VT-04.

cases.input: Update Your Billing Information
cases.note: Title case heading with different words than the library example. Tests whether the agent checks capitalization character by character.

cases.input: Update your billing information
cases.note: Same text in sentence case.

cases.input: Connect your Google Calendar
cases.note: Edge case: 'Google Calendar' is capitalized because it's a proper noun, not because it's title case. The agent should not flag this.

cases.input: New project
cases.note: Under the revised ACT-01, noun phrases are acceptable for navigation labels and section headings. 'New project' as a label should pass. Only fails if it's clearly a button CTA.

cases.input: Start a new project
cases.note: Verb-led version of the same action.

cases.input: Process payment
cases.note: 'Process' is vague. Tests whether the agent flags verbs beyond just 'submit.'

cases.input: You don't have permission to edit this document.
cases.note: Tells the user what they can't do without offering an alternative. Different scenario than file size limits.

cases.input: You can view this document. To edit, ask the owner for access.
cases.note: Same limitation framed as an alternative.

cases.input: To get started: create an account, setting up your profile, and then you should add a payment method.
cases.note: Three different grammatical patterns in one list: imperative, gerund, then 'you should.' Subtler than the library example.

cases.input: To get started: create an account, set up your profile, and add a payment method.
cases.note: Consistent imperative verbs throughout.

cases.input: Based on your current subscription level, which was selected when you originally signed up, you are limited to 3 projects at a time.
cases.note: The key info (3 project limit) is buried at the end behind unnecessary context.

cases.input: You can track orders, manage returns and contact support from your account page.
cases.note: Missing comma before 'and contact support.' Different list items than the library example.

cases.input: You can track orders, manage returns, and contact support from your account page.
cases.note: Same sentence with the Oxford comma included.

cases.case_id: GRM-01 novel two items not three
cases.input: You can track orders and manage returns from your account page.
cases.note: Only two items — no Oxford comma needed. Tests whether the agent over-applies the rule.

cases.input: Reporting & analytics
cases.note: Short label with an ampersand. Could be mistaken for a product name, but it's generic copy.

cases.input: Powered by AT&T
cases.note: Ampersand is part of a brand name. The agent should not flag this.

cases.input: You have two new notifications and five pending requests.
cases.note: Spells out small numbers instead of using numerals. Different numbers than the library example.

cases.case_id: GRM-05 novel number starts sentence pass
cases.input: Twelve users are currently online.
cases.note: Spelled out because it starts a sentence. Tests the exception in the rule.

cases.input: Getting started is a breeze.
cases.note: Different idiom than 'piece of cake.' Tests whether the agent recognizes a range of idioms.

cases.input: Getting started is simple.
cases.note: Same idea without the idiom.

cases.input: Since you updated your settings, your old preferences have been cleared.
cases.note: 'Since' used to mean 'because.' The rule specifically calls this out, but the example only covers 'once.'

cases.input: Because you updated your settings, your old preferences have been cleared.
cases.note: 'Because' is unambiguous.

cases.input: Add members to your workspace. Invite colleagues to collaborate. Share your team hub with others.
cases.note: 'Members/colleagues/others' and 'workspace/collaborate/team hub' are synonym chains. Different terms than the library campaign example.

cases.input: Upload failed. Try again later.
cases.note: Terse, unhelpful error with no empathy or explanation. Less robotic than 'Error code 408' but still cold.

cases.input: Your file couldn't be uploaded right now. Check your connection and try again — your file is still ready to go.
cases.note: Empathetic, reassuring, and actionable.

cases.input: Your subscription renews on the 1st of each month and you can cancel anytime from your account settings which you can find under the main menu.
cases.note: Three ideas chained: renewal date, cancellation policy, and navigation instructions. Different topic than the CSV export example.

cases.input: Customers who wish to update their billing details should navigate to the Account section.
cases.note: Uses 'customers' instead of 'you.' More formal third person than the library 'Users can change their password' example.

cases.input: 5 day streak
cases.note: Hyphenless numeric compound modifier — JS+Python must both flag.

cases.input: 5-day streak
cases.note: Hyphenated numeric compound modifier — JS+Python must both pass.

cases.input: one time offer ends soon
cases.note: Spelled-out compound modifier without hyphen — JS+Python must both flag.

cases.input: Get the one-time offer
cases.note: Spelled-out compound modifier with hyphen — both pass.

cases.case_id: PARITY CON-02 safe phrase: see all
cases.input: See all
cases.note: Industry-standard CTA pattern — must be in safe-phrase allowlist.

cases.case_id: PARITY CON-02 safe phrase: sign in
cases.input: Sign In
cases.note: Industry-standard CTA — title-case acceptable.

cases.input: Hello  world
cases.note: Two spaces in a row — both must catch.

cases.input: Click click here to continue
cases.note: Repeated 'click' — both must catch.

cases.input: URGENT ACTION REQUIRED IMMEDIATELY
cases.note: All-caps shouting — both must catch.

cases.input: Each user should update his settings
cases.note: Gendered pronoun without context — both must catch.

cases.input: Welcome!!
cases.note: Multiple exclamations — both must catch.

cases.input: click here
cases.note: Vague link text — both must catch.
